claims_verified.claim: O_CREAT|O_EXCL fails EEXIST (not ELOOP) when target already exists as a symlink, so the symlink itself is never followed even before O_NOFOLLOW is considered
Path('.md').suffix is '' in Python pathlib, so the suffix check alone (not the stem=="" clause) already rejects a bare '.md' target
No guard-check path leaves a partial/empty file behind on refusal — the O_CREAT|O_EXCL open is the last step before write, with no fallible guard logic between a successful create and the write
claims_verified.evidence: report_path.py:106-137, all ReportPathError raises occur before the open call or via _refuse before any file is created

claims_verified.claim: Uncaught OSError from write() (e.g. ENOSPC) bubbling out of main() instead of a clean one-line stderr message is consistent with this module's existing error-handling convention, not a regression introduced by this diff
claims_verified.evidence: cli.py: only ValueError, DockerUnavailable, and ReportPathError are caught anywhere in the file; all other OSError-class failures already bubble uncaught

claims_verified.claim: O_NOFOLLOW mechanism genuinely mirrors orchestrator/queue.py rather than reinventing it
claims_verified.evidence: queue.py:104,137,148,156,178 use the identical O_EXCL/O_NOFOLLOW/ELOOP pattern

test_assessment: Independently re-traced every guard branch (suffix, confinement resolution, O_EXCL/O_NOFOLLOW create, force-path fstat-before-truncate, dir/fifo/symlink refusals) against concrete attack inputs — dangling symlink, self-referential symlink, missing parent, full-disk mid-write, dead '.md' stem edge case — and found no exploitable escape beyond the already-documented, spec-accepted parent-symlink-swap TOCTOU window. Test suite covers all stated acceptance criteria; no gaps that would let untrusted state through unverified.
residual_risks: Same TOCTOU window already flagged by rw1: realpath(parent) is resolved before the O_NOFOLLOW open, so a component of the resolved parent could theoretically be swapped to an outside-root symlink between the two steps. Matches the accepted queue.py posture and the spec's stated threat model (sibling worktrees, not a racing adversary).

A write() failure mid-operation (e.g. ENOSPC) is not caught by the ReportPathError handler and propagates as an uncaught exception/traceback rather than a clean stderr line; this matches the rest of the module's existing exception-handling convention and isn't unique to this diff, so not treated as a regression.
